preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 48%, a substantial edge over France's 22% win probability. This represents a gap of 26 percentage points, positioning Spain as the clear favorite in this neutral-venue encounter.

The forecast shape reflects a competitive but tilted match. The draw carries meaningful probability at 30%, suggesting the teams are not dramatically separated in underlying strength. However, the model's 26-point Spain advantage indicates a meaningful gulf in expected performance. Spain's win probability more than doubles France's, though France retains a non-trivial path to victory.

The model and market diverge significantly. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price France substantially higher—around 40%—than the model's 22%, a gap of roughly 18 percentage points. Conversely, both market sources price Spain lower at 30%, some 18 points below the model. Draw probability aligns tightly across all sources near 30%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these conflicting assessments; traders and statistical models weight available information differently, and this gap warrants attention from users forming their own view.
